title: UI interaction decision tree for AI coding agents
description: A prioritized, context-aware rule set for selecting common UI interaction patterns. Designed for use in Cursor rules, prompts, or local retrieval context.
schema_standard: JSON; embedded input/response schemas use JSON Schema Draft 2020-12 conventions
principles: Choose components from the user's goal before visual preference.
Treat numeric thresholds as defaults; contextual overrides outrank counts.
Keep essential information and consequential choices visible.
Prefer native semantic elements; custom widgets inherit full accessibility obligations.
Do not turn read-only tabular information into an interactive grid.
Do not hide primary actions or task-critical help.
Insert review and confirmation for consequential AI actions.

cursor_instructions.purpose: Use this file before generating, refactoring, or reviewing interaction-heavy UI.
agent_directive: First populate input_context from the feature requirements and current UI.
When rules conflict, choose the highest-priority contextual override over a generic count threshold.
Always include the referenced requirements in implementation and review comments.
Return assumptions or request missing context when a recommendation affects accessibility, data loss, external actions, or high-impact workflows.
cursor_instructions.suggested_cursor_rule_trigger: Apply whenever implementing forms, controls, collections, navigation, dialogs, feedback states, AI-generated actions, or accessibility reviews.

condition_operator_grammar.in: Value is one member of an allowed set: {"risk_level":{"in":["irreversible","high_impact"]}}.
condition_operator_grammar.not_in: Value is absent from a disallowed set.
condition_operator_grammar.min_max: Numeric inclusive comparison; use min, max, or both.
condition_operator_grammar.any: Logical OR across child condition objects; all sibling properties outside any are additionally required.
condition_operator_grammar.any_missing: A rule matches when one or more named context properties are absent.

input_context_schema.description: Facts an AI agent should identify before recommending UI components. Omit unknown optional fields and disclose assumptions.

evaluation_algorithm.action: Normalize the proposed UI task into input_context fields. Do not infer consequential fields silently.
Apply global constraints and meta rules before choosing a component.
Evaluate matching rules in descending priority; contextual override rules beat numeric defaults.
Return one primary recommendation, at most two alternatives, rationale, assumptions, requirements, and states.
For custom composite widgets, include implementation obligations from A11Y_COMPOSITE_PATTERN.
For user-visible collections or actions, specify empty, loading, error, success, and permission states when applicable.
For AI-initiated or high-risk actions, insert review/confirmation before execution.

A11Y_VISIBLE_LABEL.requirement: Inputs have a persistent visible label; placeholder text is not a substitute for a label.

A11Y_GROUP_LABEL.requirement: Grouped controls expose a group name using semantic grouping such as fieldset and legend where applicable.

A11Y_KEYBOARD.requirement: All interactive behavior is operable with keyboard alone and preserves logical focus order.

A11Y_FOCUS_VISIBLE.requirement: Interactive controls have a clear visible focus indicator.

A11Y_SEMANTIC_NATIVE.requirement: Prefer native semantic HTML over custom ARIA widgets when native behavior fulfills the need.

A11Y_NO_COLOR_ONLY.requirement: State, errors, selection, and status are not communicated by color alone.

A11Y_TOUCH_TARGET.requirement: Interactive targets are large and spaced enough for pointer and touch input.

A11Y_ERROR_ASSOCIATION.requirement: Validation errors identify the field, describe recovery, and are programmatically associated with the input.

A11Y_DYNAMIC_ANNOUNCE.requirement: Meaningful dynamic results or status changes are announced appropriately without excessive interruption.

A11Y_DIALOG_FOCUS.requirement: Modal dialogs move focus inside on open, keep focus within while open, support Escape where appropriate, and return focus on close.

A11Y_TABLE_HEADERS.requirement: Static data tables include a caption where useful and correctly associated row/column headers using scope or headers/id for complex headers.

A11Y_SORT_ANNOUNCE.requirement: Sortable tabular views expose sort state and announce changes to assistive technology.

A11Y_COMPOSITE_PATTERN.requirement: Custom composite widgets implement the full expected ARIA pattern and keyboard behavior, not roles alone.

A11Y_REDUCED_MOTION.requirement: Animations do not block comprehension and respect reduced-motion preferences.

CONTENT_PLAIN_LANGUAGE.requirement: Labels describe the outcome in concise user language; avoid internal system terminology.

CONTENT_ACTION_VERB.requirement: Action labels begin with a specific verb describing the result, such as Save, Delete, Send, or Download.

CONTENT_ERROR_RECOVERY.requirement: Error messages explain what happened and the next available recovery action.

CONTENT_DESTRUCTIVE_SCOPE.requirement: Destructive confirmations name the affected object, scope, and irreversibility or recovery method.

AGENT_NO_INVENTED_COMPLEXITY.requirement: Do not introduce a complex widget when a native control or visible content solves the task.

AGENT_STATE_ALL_STATES.requirement: Specify loading, empty, error, permission, and success states for generated UI.

AGENT_RISK_REVIEW.requirement: External, irreversible, destructive, or high-impact AI actions require a review or confirmation step before execution.

AGENT_DECLARE_ASSUMPTIONS.requirement: When context is insufficient for a high-confidence recommendation, return assumptions and missing variables rather than silently choosing.

text_input.summary: Single-line entry for short or structured freeform values.

textarea.summary: Multi-line entry for longer authored responses.

search_input.summary: Input optimized for finding results by user-supplied query.
search_input.preferred_semantics: form with labelled search input and submit button

numeric_input.preferred_semantics: input with appropriate inputmode/type and label

range_slider.summary: Approximate selection on a continuum when relative position matters.

date_input.summary: Typed date for memorable or known dates.
date_input.preferred_semantics: grouped date fields or validated text/date input

date_picker.summary: Calendar-assisted date selection where calendar context matters.
date_picker.preferred_semantics: labelled input with accessible date picker enhancement

date_range_picker.summary: Selecting related start and end dates.
date_range_picker.preferred_semantics: paired labelled inputs with accessible calendar enhancement

time_input.summary: Known or freely chosen time entry.

time_slot_list.preferred_semantics: radio group or selectable buttons with single-select semantics

file_input.summary: User supplies one or more files.

checkbox.summary: Independent opt-in, acknowledgment, or inclusion within submitted form.

switch.summary: Binary setting that takes effect immediately and has an on/off state.
switch.preferred_semantics: native checkbox styled/announced as switch or established switch pattern

radio_group.summary: Exactly one choice with all choices visible.

radio_cards.summary: Exactly one rich/described/visual choice, visibly comparable.
radio_cards.preferred_semantics: radio inputs represented as selectable tiles

segmented_control.summary: Very small mutually exclusive view or mode switch with short parallel labels.
segmented_control.preferred_semantics: radio-group semantics for selection; buttons only if triggering commands

select.summary: Compact single-choice list with brief labels and low comparison need.

combobox.summary: Searchable/typeahead choice from a known option set.
combobox.preferred_semantics: ARIA-compliant combobox backed by listbox options

autocomplete_search.summary: Search-first selection from a very large, dynamic, or remote corpus.

checkbox_group.summary: Multiple visible choices from a manageable set.

multi_select_combobox.summary: Search, add, and remove several items from a large known set.
multi_select_combobox.preferred_semantics: accessible multi-selection pattern with selected tokens

filter_chips.summary: Visible summary and removal of active filters.
filter_chips.preferred_semantics: buttons for removal; no chip-only hidden filters

sort_control.summary: Change ordering of results without filtering.
sort_control.preferred_semantics: labelled select/menu describing sort criterion and order

search_filters.summary: Find and narrow a collection using search plus refinements.

list.summary: Scan ordered or repeated items without column comparison.

card_grid.summary: Browse rich distinct objects with preview metadata or imagery.

table.summary: Read and compare consistently structured values by row and column.

data_grid.preferred_semantics: ARIA grid pattern only when required

tree.preferred_semantics: treeview only when interaction benefits outweigh complexity

treegrid.summary: Interact with hierarchical records containing aligned fields.
treegrid.preferred_semantics: treegrid pattern only for true hierarchical interactive table

chart_with_data.summary: Understand patterns while preserving inspectable values.

progress_indicator.preferred_semantics: progress element or accessible labelled indicator

skeleton.summary: Reserved layout while predictable content is loading.
skeleton.preferred_semantics: non-interactive placeholders hidden appropriately from AT

empty_state.summary: Explain why a collection is empty and provide next action.

inline_validation.preferred_semantics: associated error text adjacent to field

error_summary.summary: Summarize submission errors and link to affected fields.
error_summary.preferred_semantics: alert region with focus on submit failure

alert.summary: Persistent important page or section status.

toast.summary: Transient low-risk confirmation that does not require response.

modal_dialog.summary: Blocking decision or focused required task.

nonmodal_panel.summary: Contextual support without blocking main task.

disclosure.summary: Reveal optional or supplemental content inline.

accordion.summary: Independently reveal multiple optional content sections.

tabs.summary: Switch among peer panels in the same local context.

button.summary: Trigger a command or state change.

primary_button.summary: Most important forward or commit command in a local task.

menu_button.summary: Reveal a compact list of related actions.
menu_button.preferred_semantics: button with menu pattern when keyboard interaction is warranted

overflow_menu.summary: Keep infrequent item actions available without visual clutter.

toolbar.summary: Group recurring controls that operate on a workspace or selection.
toolbar.preferred_semantics: toolbar pattern when keyboard navigation is implemented

split_button.summary: Primary command with closely related alternate modes.

link.summary: Navigate to another destination or resource.

breadcrumbs.summary: Communicate location and ancestor paths in hierarchy.
breadcrumbs.preferred_semantics: nav aria-label Breadcrumb with ordered links

in_page_navigation.summary: Jump to sections on a long page.

pagination.summary: Move through addressable pages of results.
pagination.preferred_semantics: navigation with links/buttons and current page

load_more.summary: Append more browsable results while preserving current context.

infinite_scroll.preferred_semantics: feed pattern only with navigation/access alternatives

tooltip.summary: A brief supplementary message shown on focus or hover; never required for completion.
tooltip.preferred_semantics: accessible tooltip pattern associated with trigger

prefer_native_semantic_control.summary: Replace unnecessary custom widget behavior with the most fitting native semantic control.

review_then_confirm.summary: Present the proposed consequence for review and require explicit user confirmation before executing.

request_confirmation_with_assumptions.summary: Surface uncertainty and assumptions, then request confirmation before consequential execution.

editable_preview.summary: Display AI-generated content as a reviewable, editable draft before sending, submitting, or publishing.

diff_preview.summary: Display meaningful changes against the existing content before applying them.

show_by_default.summary: Render required information visibly without disclosure interaction.

show_side_by_side_or_sections.summary: Expose content simultaneously for comparison rather than hiding panels behind tabs.

inline_help_text.summary: Keep completion-critical help persistently visible in context.

root.question: What does the user need to accomplish and what consequence follows from the interaction?

branches.label: Search, filter, sort, and bulk select

rule_resolution.note: The decision tree indexes rules by task branch; the prioritized rules are executable decision leaves.

rules.rationale: The component family cannot be reliably chosen until the user's primary task is identified.
rules.agent_output_notes: Ask for or infer user_goal only when low-risk. Return candidate families if rendering must proceed.

rules.rationale: Native HTML controls typically provide more reliable keyboard, focus, and platform behavior than bespoke widgets.
AI execution should be reviewable before it changes external systems or consequential records.
Consequential or irreversible actions warrant a focused confirmation decision before execution.
Low-confidence consequential choices must expose assumptions and request confirmation.
Information needed for the current task should not be hidden behind interaction.
Sorting is an ordering command, not a generic field selection question.
Required instructions must remain visible rather than depend on hover, focus, or discovery.
Keep rich or consequential options visible so users can read and compare before selection.
A local task should expose its primary forward or commit action clearly.
A submission with multiple errors needs a summary plus inline field messages.
Meaningful modifications need a before/after view or change summary before applying.
Tabs hide values required for direct comparison.
Two meaningful alternatives are choices, not merely an on/off state.
For easily recoverable destructive actions, immediate execution with undo can reduce needless interruption.
Navigation to a resource or page is semantically a link.
A command or state change is semantically a button action.
Generated content that may be sent, submitted, or published must be reviewed before commitment.
A grid is warranted only when the table behaves as an interactive workspace requiring directional navigation.
When users must decide before continuation, focus should be contained in a dialog.
Field-specific errors need proximity and an explicit recovery instruction.
Checkboxes fit opt-in or acknowledgment values committed as part of form submission.
A switch conveys an immediate on/off state change.
Interactive hierarchical records with aligned fields require a specialized treegrid pattern.
Bulk actions require explicit record selection and visible scope.
rules.agent_output_notes: Treat row checkboxes as selection controls; display selected count and action scope.

rules.rationale: Aligned columns enable exact comparison of consistent record attributes.
Required ordered stages should be represented as a process rather than tabs.
Dynamic or unbounded values require query-driven retrieval and result feedback.
Known datasets may benefit from both typing and scrolling regardless of raw count.
Static tabular information belongs in a semantic table, not an interactive grid.
Searching is an information-retrieval task with query submission and result feedback.
Stable page positions support finding, comparing, revisiting, and acting on records.
Users must see and remove active constraints without hunting for them.
When users need to inspect choices while selecting multiple values, filterable visible choices preserve comparison.
A navigable hierarchy can use a tree only when expansion/collapse and directional navigation materially aid the task.
File selection should use platform file upload behavior with clear constraints.
rules.agent_output_notes: State accepted formats, maximum size, multi-file behavior, upload/progress/failure states.

rules.rationale: Tabs show one of several peer panels within the same local object or context.
Visible checkboxes let users scan and select several options independently.
Search-and-add interaction avoids forcing users to scan a long multi-choice list.
A small exclusive choice set can be exposed without forcing users to open a menu.
Frequently used workspace controls benefit from predictable grouping and keyboard behavior.
Alternatives requiring attribute comparison should align information in a table.
Filters narrow a collection by known attributes.
Important persistent status should remain perceivable in context.
Start/end date relationships should be captured together with validation of ordering.
When only certain times are available, users should choose from valid slots instead of encountering errors.
Search is useful when users can name or describe their target.
Ordering options should be explicitly labelled and distinct from filtering.
A visualization helps reveal patterns, but inspectable values and a text summary remain necessary.
Calendar context supports scheduling decisions.
A disclosure reduces noise while keeping supplemental detail available on demand.
A small set of parallel local views or modes can be immediately toggled.
Users should type familiar dates such as birthdates rather than navigate a calendar.
Infrequent secondary commands may be grouped without hiding the primary action.
A slider works when relative position is meaningful and exact precision is not required.
A multi-page section benefits from visible local navigation and current location.
Multiple independent supplemental sections can be collapsed individually.
Failed loading requires an error explanation and retry or recovery route.
Exact numeric entry should not require imprecise pointer manipulation.
A user-specified time is best entered directly.
Cards support recognition and browsing when objects include preview imagery, descriptions, or actions.
Deep interior pages benefit from ancestor context and upward navigation.
Lists provide compact scanability for repeated items read in order.
A brief non-blocking confirmation is appropriate for low-risk completed actions.
Appending results supports browsing while preserving an explicit control and page footer.
No-result states should help users recover by clearing filters or revising queries.
Known progress should communicate completion status.
Multi-sentence responses need a resizable multi-line writing surface.
First-use empty states should explain value and expose the creation action.
Simple alternatives can be presented compactly as a list.
Known completion progress should expose current status toward completion.
Interactive supporting content needs a stable focusable container rather than a tooltip.
Short freeform values fit a single-line input.
Primary destinations need persistent top-level orientation.
At very large scale the task becomes finding a value rather than visually scanning choices.
A searchable/typeahead option chooser reduces long-menu scrolling.
A moderate single-select list with concise labels can be compactly presented.
A split button is acceptable only when a safe default command dominates and alternatives are tightly related.
A tooltip is limited to brief supplementary information and must work on focus as well as hover.
Long pages with distinct sections benefit from direct section jumps.
Indeterminate short waits need succinct activity feedback.
Skeleton placeholders preserve predictable layout while content loads.
Compact status labels support scanning when their wording conveys meaning independent of color.
Continuous loading is reserved for feed consumption, not goal-directed retrieval.

USWDS_COMBOBOX.title: U.S. Web Design System — Combo box
